title: Cohort Analysis Without SQL Using Filters and Charts
summary: Cohort questions—how March signups behaved in month two—do not require a warehouse if exports include signup dates.

content.summary: Cohort questions—how March signups behaved in month two—do not require a warehouse if exports include signup dates.

body.text: Business users defer cohort analysis to BI teams when filters and charts could answer many cases. Filter signup_date to March, chart subsequent activity by weeks-since-signup or status transitions. Save cohort rules with clear names: Cohort-2026-03-Active-Week4.

body.caption: Calculator and financial data analysis. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Filter signup_date to March, chart subsequent activity by weeks-since-signup or status transitions.
Save cohort rules with clear names: Cohort-2026-03-Active-Week4.

items: Ensure export grain is one row per customer or transaction as needed.
Align activity date columns for follow-on charts.
Document cohort definition in rule names.

body.text: Small cohorts swing wildly—note sample size in slides.
Repeat monthly with fresh uploads and same saved logic.
Datilio filters turn cohort slices into repeatable charts without SQL.
